SEARCH
Package com.unimib.assignment3.service

Class TaskService

java.lang.Object
com.unimib.assignment3.service.TaskService
@Service public class TaskService extends Object
Service class for managing Task entities.

Provides operations for task creation, state management, employee assignment, and advanced queries regarding task complexity and scheduling.

    Description

    Retrieves tasks filtered by their current state.

  • Constructor Details

    • TaskService

      public TaskService()

  • Method Details

    • createTask

      @Transactional public Task createTask(TaskState initialState)
      Creates a new task with an initial state and sets relevant dates.
      Parameters:
      initialState - the starting state of the task
      Returns:
      the newly created task entity

    • saveTask

      @Transactional public Task saveTask(Task task)
      Saves or updates a task in the database.
      Parameters:
      task - the task entity to save
      Returns:
      the persisted task entity

    • assignEmployeeToTask

      @Transactional public void assignEmployeeToTask(Long taskId, Long employeeId)
      Assigns an employee to a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the ID of the task
      employeeId - the ID of the employee to assign
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the task or employee is not found
      IllegalStateException - if the task is already completed or the employee is already assigned

    • removeEmployeeFromTask

      @Transactional public void removeEmployeeFromTask(Long taskId, Long employeeId)
      Removes an employee from a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the ID of the task
      employeeId - the ID of the employee to remove
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the task or employee is not found

    • changeTaskState

      @Transactional public Task changeTaskState(Long taskId, TaskState newState)
      Transitions a task to a new state and updates dates accordingly.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the ID of the task to update
      newState - the target state
      Returns:
      the updated task entity
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the transition is logically invalid

    • resetTask

      @Transactional public void resetTask(Long taskId)
      Resets a task to the 'TO_BE_STARTED' state and clears all associated dates.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the ID of the task to reset

    • getTasksByState

      public List<Task> getTasksByState(TaskState state)
      Retrieves tasks filtered by their current state.
      Parameters:
      state - the task state to filter by
      Returns:
      a list of matching tasks

    • getTasksByEmployee

      public List<Task> getTasksByEmployee(Employee employee)
      Retrieves all tasks assigned to a specific employee.
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee entity
      Returns:
      a list of tasks assigned to the employee

    • getUnassignedTasks

      public List<Task> getUnassignedTasks()
      Retrieves all tasks that have no employees assigned.
      Returns:
      a list of unassigned tasks

    • countTasksByState

      public long countTasksByState(TaskState state)
      Counts the total number of tasks in a specific state.
      Parameters:
      state - the task state to count
      Returns:
      the count of tasks

    • getComplexTasks

      public List<Task> getComplexTasks(int numEmployees)
      Retrieves 'complex' tasks that have more than a specified number of employees assigned.
      Parameters:
      numEmployees - the employee threshold
      Returns:
      a list of complex tasks

    • getTaskById

      public Task getTaskById(Long id)
      Finds a task by its unique ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the task ID
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the task if found

    • getAllTasks

      public List<Task> getAllTasks()
      Retrieves all tasks in the system.
      Returns:
      a list of all tasks

    • deleteTask

      @Transactional public void deleteTask(Long id)
      Deletes a task from the system by ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the task to delete

    • isEmployeeAssigned

      public boolean isEmployeeAssigned(Long taskId, Long employeeId)
      Checks if a specific employee is assigned to a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task ID
      employeeId - the employee ID
      Returns:
      true if the employee is assigned, false otherwise

    • getTasksByStateWithEmployees

      public List<Task> getTasksByStateWithEmployees(TaskState state)
      Retrieves tasks in a specific state that have at least one employee assigned.
      Parameters:
      state - the task state
      Returns:
      a list of matching tasks

    • getEmployeeCountPerTask

      public Integer getEmployeeCountPerTask(Long taskId)
      Gets the total number of employees assigned to a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task ID
      Returns:
      the count of assigned employees

    • setAssignedEmployees

      @Transactional public void setAssignedEmployees(Long taskId, List<Employee> employees)
      Sets the list of employees assigned to a specific task. Checks that the list and the employees within it are not null.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the ID of the task
      employees - the list of employees to assign
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the list is null or contains null elements

    • getTasksByStateAndEmployeeCount

      public List<Task> getTasksByStateAndEmployeeCount(TaskState state, int numEmployees)
      Retrieves tasks that match a specific state and an exact employee count.
      Parameters:
      state - the task state
      numEmployees - the exact number of employees required
      Returns:
      a list of matching tasks

    • getTasksByTeam

      public List<Task> getTasksByTeam(Long idTeam)
      Retrieves all tasks belonging to a specific team.
      Parameters:
      idTeam - the team ID
      Returns:
      a list of tasks for that team

    • setTaskStartDate

      @Transactional public Task setTaskStartDate(Long taskId, LocalDate startDate)
      Sets the start date for a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task ID
      startDate - the new start date
      Returns:
      the updated task entity

    • setTaskEndDate

      @Transactional public Task setTaskEndDate(Long taskId, LocalDate endDate)
      Sets the end date for a specific task.
      Parameters:
      taskId - the task ID
      endDate - the new end date
      Returns:
      the updated task entity
